headline: Google and Blackstone Join Forces to Topple Nvidia’s AI Cloud Dominance
summary: Alphabet stock is nearing a $5 trillion market cap—and the Google parent hasn’t gotten there just by standing still.  The latest news: Google and  Blackstone  announced plans to create a new AI cloud company using the tech giant’s chips.  The joint venture will create a new U.S.-based company offering data center capacity and Google’s Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) chips as a “compute-as-a-service” offering, the companies said.

headline: Blackstone Announces Joint Venture with Google to Create New TPU Cloud
summary: NEW YORK, May 19, 2026--Blackstone (NYSE: BX) today announced a joint venture with Google to create a new U.S.-based company that will offer efficient data center capacity, operations, networking, and Google Cloud's Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) as a compute-as-a-service offering. The company will give customers another option to access cloud TPUs in addition to using them through Google Cloud.

raw.headline: Blackstone Announces Joint Venture with Google to Create New TPU Cloud
raw.summary: NEW YORK, May 19, 2026--Blackstone (NYSE: BX) today announced a joint venture with Google to create a new U.S.-based company that will offer efficient data center capacity, operations, networking, and Google Cloud's Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) as a compute-as-a-service offering. The company will give customers another option to access cloud TPUs in addition to using them through Google Cloud.

headline: Google, Blackstone plan AI cloud venture with $5 billion backing, WSJ reports
summary: Alphabet's Google and Blackstone plans to launch an artificial-intelligence cloud company using Google's specialized ‌chips, the Wall Street Journal reported on ‌Monday, citing sources.  Blackstone, the world's largest alternative asset manager, is expected ​to contribute $5 billion in equity and hold a majority stake in the unnamed U.S. venture, with a deal expected to be announced in a few hours, the ‌report said.  Google will ⁠supply hardware, including its specialized chips known as Tensor Processing Units, or TPUs, as ⁠well as software and services to the venture, the WSJ reported.

Google will ⁠supply hardware, including its specialized chips known as Tensor Processing Units, or TPUs, as ⁠well as software and services to the venture, the WSJ reported.
